business.headline: Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

Live: Iran brings 71-member delegation to Islamabad talks led by GhalibafUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran's delegation, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, arrived in Islamabad early Saturday for high-stakes talks with the US aimed at ending weeks of conflict. The visit comes amid growing urgency to stabilise the region, with President Donald Trump warning of intensified strikes if no agreement is reached. Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has called the talks a "make-or-break moment" after announcing a two-week ceasefire earlier this week. According to Tasnim news agency, the Iranian delegation includes around 70 members, comprising technical experts in economic, security and political fields. The talks are expected to begin after the arrival of the US delegation led by Vice President JD Vance, even as tensions linked to Israel-Hezbollah fighting continue to cast a shadow. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.
